cover.title: your agents don't share a memory
cover.sub: each one learns your system alone, then drifts. here's the failure, and the fix.

slides.title: the same lesson, twice
slides.body: agent A learns the rollback the hard way. agent B, an hour later, re-derives it and lands on the version you already killed. no shared memory, no shared truth.

pairs.broke: every agent keeps its own private copy of the truth
pairs.fix: one store. every read and write goes through it.

slides.title: the hard part isn't the store
slides.body: it's the discipline of routing a whole team's agents through one source of truth, every time. the tool is easy. the habit is the work.

cta.title: where does your fleet's truth live
cta.sub: if the answer is 'in each agent's head,' they're already drifting apart.
